encounter.reason: Initial rheumatology consultation, RA confirmed and methotrexate started
encounter.location: Bay Area Rheumatology Associates, Oakland, CA

screening.name: tender joint count
screening.description: Metacarpophalangeal joints, proximal interphalangeal joints, both wrists

screening.name: swollen joint count
screening.description: Metacarpophalangeal joints, proximal interphalangeal joints, both wrists

screening.name: patient global assessment
screening.description: Patient-reported global assessment of disease activity; moderate-to-high patient-perceived disease activity indicating significant functional impairment and symptoms

screening.name: disease activity score 28-CRP
screening.description: Composite disease activity score combining tender joint count, swollen joint count, C-reactive protein level, and patient global assessment; moderate-to-high disease activity indicating active synovitis and need for DMARD initiation

conditions.name: seropositive rheumatoid arthritis
conditions.description: Bilateral hands and wrists (MCPs, PIPs, both wrists involved on exam). Positive rheumatoid factor (84 IU/mL), strongly positive anti-CCP (112 units). Moderate-to-high disease activity with DAS28-CRP 5.1, 8 tender joints, 6 swollen joints, patient global assessment 58/100. Clinical presentation, positive serologies, inflammatory markers, and physical exam findings consistent with active synovitis and new diagnosis at this encounter.

medications.name: methotrexate 15 mg oral tablet
medications.reason: seropositive rheumatoid arthritis; early DMARD therapy to reduce risk of permanent erosive joint damage

medications.name: folic acid 1 mg oral tablet
medications.reason: co-therapy with methotrexate to reduce methotrexate side effects including hematologic and gastrointestinal toxicity

document_references.description: Initial rheumatology consultation documenting chief complaint and history of present illness (bilateral hand and wrist pain, morning stiffness >90 minutes, functional difficulty, several months of symptoms), review of outside workup (positive rheumatoid factor, strongly positive anti-CCP, elevated inflammatory markers from March 2023 PCP labs), vital signs, physical examination (alert and in no acute distress; bilateral MCP/PIP/wrist synovitis with tenderness; 8 tender joints, 6 swollen joints), disease activity assessment (patient global assessment 58/100, DAS28-CRP 5.1), baseline safety monitoring labs (CBC, renal function, liver function, hepatitis B screening), assessment of seropositive rheumatoid arthritis newly confirmed with moderate-to-high disease activity, and plan including methotrexate 15 mg once weekly and folic acid 1 mg daily with counseling on risks/benefits, monitoring requirements, alcohol limitation, teratogenicity and contraception discussion, and follow-up in approximately 8 weeks.
